index.title: The multifaceted effects of semaglutide: exploring its broad applications

evidence_records.direct_quote: Semaglutide, a GLP-1 receptor agonist, is FDA-approved for managing type 2 diabetes (T2D) and reducing cardiovascular risk.
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide is FDA-approved for managing type 2 diabetes and reducing cardiovascular risk, with off-label use growing for weight management.

evidence_records.direct_quote: the semaglutide group exhibited a statistically significant decrease in weight (−10.2%) and waist-to-hip ratio (−6.9%)
evidence_records.statement: In the SELECT trial of 17,604 patients with preexisting cardiovascular disease, semaglutide produced a 10.2% weight decrease after 208 weeks.

evidence_records.direct_quote: A meta-analysis of 13 randomized controlled trials involving 5838 patients demonstrated significant absolute weight reduction
evidence_records.statement: A meta-analysis of 13 RCTs with 5,838 patients demonstrated significant absolute weight reduction with semaglutide.

index.title: SELECT Trial Analysis Shows Semaglutide's Heart Benefits Extend ...

evidence_records.direct_quote: semaglutide resulted in a 20% reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events (MACE)
evidence_records.statement: In SELECT, semaglutide produced a 20% reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events in patients with obesity and established CVD but not diabetes.

evidence_records.direct_quote: approximately 33% of semaglutide’s cardioprotective benefit was explained by reductions in waist circumference (HR, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.77–0.97)
evidence_records.statement: Approximately 33% of semaglutide's cardioprotective benefit was explained by waist circumference reductions (HR 0.86, 95% CI 0.77-0.97).

index.title: Semaglutide-associated kidney injury

evidence_records.direct_quote: later in non-diabetic obese patients with the semaglutide effects on cardiovascular outcomes in people with overweight or obesity (SELECT) trial
evidence_records.statement: The SELECT trial showed cardiovascular benefit of semaglutide in non-diabetic obese patients.

evidence_records.direct_quote: a serum creatinine (SCr) of 6.72 mg/dL (baseline 1.2–1.5 mg/dL) detected during a routine 4-month laboratory examination. After the initiation
evidence_records.statement: Case 1: a 68-year-old woman developed AIN after weekly semaglutide 0.25 mg; serum creatinine rose from baseline 1.2-1.5 to 6.72 mg/dL.

index.title: Exploring GLP-1 Weight Loss Drugs: Affordability and Accessibility

evidence_records.direct_quote: semaglutide reduced body weight by 15% in certain patient groups
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide reduced body weight by 15% in certain patient groups in clinical research.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Weight regain occurred in STEP 4 after semaglutide therapy ended, thus revealing the necessity for sustained patient treatment
evidence_records.statement: STEP 4 showed weight regain after semaglutide therapy ended, indicating the need for sustained treatment.

index.title: The Economics of Obesity Therapy [Ozempic, Wegovy] - Medium

evidence_records.direct_quote: there is a chronic shortage of semaglutide (the ingredient in Ozempic and Wegovy)
evidence_records.statement: There is a current chronic shortage of semaglutide, the active ingredient in both Ozempic and Wegovy.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Both groups received nutrition and exercise counseling. Thus, the true incremental benefit of semaglutide is a weight loss of — 12.5%.
evidence_records.statement: Both semaglutide and placebo groups received nutrition and exercise counseling, so the incremental semaglutide benefit is roughly 12.5% weight loss.

evidence_records.direct_quote:
Semaglutide has to be taken for life…
… Otherwise, patients fairly quickly regain weight back upon interrupting treatment.
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide must be taken for life because patients regain weight quickly after stopping treatment.

index.title: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials of Once-Weekly Semaglutide 2.4 mg for Long-Term Weight Loss

evidence_records.direct_quote: absolute body weight (WMD −12.3 kg, 95% CI −13.6 to −11.0)
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide reduced absolute body weight by a weighted mean of 12.3 kg compared with placebo.

index.title: Does Semaglutide Cause Pancreatitis? Evidence and Safety Guide

evidence_records.direct_quote: enhances glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppresses glucagon release, slows gastric emptying, and reduces appetite through central nervous system pathways
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide mechanism includes enhancing glucose-dependent insulin secretion, suppressing glucagon, slowing gastric emptying, and reducing appetite.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Rapid weight loss associated with semaglutide may paradoxically increase gallstone formation risk
evidence_records.statement: Rapid weight loss from semaglutide may paradoxically increase gallstone formation risk, a leading cause of pancreatitis.

index.title: Tirzepatide vs. semaglutide in type 2 diabetes and obesity: a systematic review and meta-analysis of metabolic efficacy, weight loss, and cardiovascular safety

evidence_records.direct_quote: Agonistas do receptor do peptídeo-1 semelhante ao glucagon (GLP-1 RA), como Semaglutida, e agonistas duais de GLP-1 e do polipeptídeo insulinotrópico dependente de glicose (GIP), como Tirzepatide
evidence_records.statement: The review compares GLP-1 RA semaglutide against dual GLP-1/GIP agonist tirzepatide in adults with T2DM and obesity.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Tirzepatide apresenta superioridade na redução de HbA1c e perda de peso em comparação à Semaglutida
evidence_records.statement: Tirzepatide showed superior HbA1c reduction and weight loss relative to semaglutide in pooled analyses.

index.title: Novel Antidiabetic Drug Approvals in 2025: Clinical Evidence, Regulatory Milestones, and Global Market Implications

evidence_records.direct_quote: major adverse cardiovascular events (HR 0.82; 95% CI, 0.68–0.98)
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide reduced major adverse cardiovascular events with hazard ratio 0.82 (95% CI 0.68 to 0.98) in the FLOW trial.

evidence_records.direct_quote: composite primary endpoint of sustained ≥ 50% eGFR decline, end-stage kidney disease, renal death, or cardiovascular death
evidence_records.statement: In FLOW, semaglutide produced a 24% reduction in the composite kidney/cardiovascular endpoint (HR 0.76, 95% CI 0.66 to 0.88, p=0.0003).

evidence_records.direct_quote: expanded the label of semaglutide (Ozempic®) to include a chronic kidney disease (CKD) protection indication
evidence_records.statement: On January 28, 2025, the FDA expanded semaglutide's label to include a chronic kidney disease protection indication in adults with type 2 diabetes and CKD.

evidence_records.statement: In overweight or obese patients without diabetes, semaglutide reduced weight by a mean of 11.41 kg versus control.

evidence_records.direct_quote: a total of 15 RCTs comparing semaglutide with placebo or other glucose-lowering drugs in weight reduction, totaling 6984 patients
evidence_records.statement: The meta-analysis included 15 RCTs totaling 6984 overweight or obese adult patients comparing semaglutide to placebo or other glucose-lowering drugs.

evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide produced a significant relative body weight reduction vs placebo of MD -10.09%.
Semaglutide users were 11.6x more likely than placebo to reach >=20% weight loss from baseline.

evidence_records.statement: Weight loss with semaglutide continued through week 65 and was sustained for up to 4 years.

evidence_records.statement: In the SELECT trial, semaglutide produced a 20% reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events in adults with preexisting CVD, overweight or obesity, without diabetes.

semaglutide 2.4 mg once weekly resulted in approximately 15% mean

evidence_records.statement: STEP 1 trial reported semaglutide 2.4 mg weekly produced approximately 15% mean weight loss at 68 weeks compared to 2-3% with placebo.

evidence_records.statement: Patients on semaglutide or tirzepatide can often target at least 15% weight loss when treatment is tolerated and accessible, versus the standard 5-10% goal.

evidence_records.statement: Gallbladder-related disorders were more common with semaglutide than placebo in the SELECT study (2.8% vs. 2.3%).

evidence_records.direct_quote: safety of once‐weekly subcutaneous semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo, beyond reduction in major adverse cardiovascular events
evidence_records.statement: The SELECT study evaluated once-weekly subcutaneous semaglutide 2.4 mg in patients with established CVD and overweight or obesity over nearly 4 years.

evidence_records.direct_quote: proportion of patients with SAEs was lower with semaglutide versus placebo (33.4% vs. 36.4%; p
evidence_records.statement: The proportion of patients with serious adverse events was lower with semaglutide versus placebo (33.4% vs. 36.4%).

index.title: Ozempic: FDA Warning Label Information - Motley Rice

evidence_records.direct_quote: Studies find that taking semaglutide can increase the risk of developing DVT by 266%
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide use is reported to increase risk of deep vein thrombosis by 266% per cited studies.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Ozempic and other diabetes and weight loss medications reduce appetite and slow digestion, which can lead to malnutrition
evidence_records.statement: Ozempic and similar GLP-1 medications may cause malnutrition by reducing appetite and slowing digestion.

evidence_records.direct_quote: The FDA approved Wegovy to treat obesity and encourage weight loss in adults on June 4, 2021
evidence_records.statement: Wegovy (semaglutide) was FDA-approved for obesity treatment in adults on June 4, 2021.

index.title: Semaglutide Effects on Cardiovascular Outcomes in People With Overweight or Obesity (SELECT)

evidence_records.direct_quote: Patients with overweight or obesity (body mass index [BMI] ≥27 kg/m2) and established CVD were randomized to receive once-weekly subcutaneous semaglutide (n = 8,803) vs. placebo (n = 8,801)
evidence_records.statement: SELECT randomized 17,604 patients with overweight or obesity (BMI ≥27) and established CVD without diabetes to weekly semaglutide 2.4 mg versus placebo.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Gastrointestinal (GI) complications: 10.0% vs. 2.0% (p < 0.001)
evidence_records.statement: Gastrointestinal complications were markedly higher with semaglutide: 10.0% versus 2.0% placebo (p<0.001).

evidence_records.direct_quote: 6.5% vs. 8.0% (hazard ratio [HR] 0.80, 95% confidence interval [CI] 0.72-0.90, p < 0.001)
evidence_records.statement: The primary MACE outcome occurred in 6.5% of semaglutide versus 8.0% of placebo patients (HR 0.80, 95% CI 0.72-0.90, p<0.001).

index.title: Semaglutide - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf

evidence_records.direct_quote: STEP 8 trial, compared subcutaneous weekly semaglutide (Wegovy®) to subcutaneous daily liraglutide, revealing semaglutide's superiority
evidence_records.statement: STEP 8 trial demonstrated that weekly subcutaneous semaglutide was superior to daily subcutaneous liraglutide in mean percentage change in body weight.

evidence_records.direct_quote: FDA has approved the use of 2.4 mg weekly subcutaneous semaglutide for chronic weight management
evidence_records.statement: The recommended maintenance dose of subcutaneous semaglutide (Wegovy) for chronic weight management is 2.4 mg weekly.

evidence_records.statement: Up to one-third of weight loss on GLP-1 medications comes from lean tissue rather than fat.

evidence_records.statement: GLP-1 users experience severe portion limits, tolerating only 4-6 oz of food per meal.

evidence_records.statement: The study confirmed GLP-1RA drugs can lower risk of heart attack, stroke and other cardiovascular concerns.

evidence_records.statement: GLP-1RA use is linked to increased gastrointestinal problems including nausea, vomiting, diarrhea and rare gastric paralysis.

evidence_records.direct_quote: novel is the potential ways GLP-1RA drugs can negatively affect the pancreas and kidneys
evidence_records.statement: The drugs carry novel potential adverse effects on the pancreas including pancreatitis risk.

index.title: Ozempic (semaglutide) EMA Product Information

evidence_records.direct_quote: The events led to treatment discontinuation in 3.9% and 5% of patients
evidence_records.statement: Gastrointestinal adverse events led to discontinuation in 3.9% and 5% of patients on semaglutide 0.5 mg and 1 mg.

evidence_records.direct_quote: approximately two-fold increase in the relative risk of developing NAION, corresponding to approximately one additional case per 10 000 person-years
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide exposure is associated with approximately two-fold increased relative risk of NAION, about one additional case per 10,000 person-years.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Nausea occurred in 17% and 19.9% of patients when treated with semaglutide 0.5 mg and 1 mg, respectively
evidence_records.statement: Nausea occurred in 17% and 19.9% of patients on semaglutide 0.5 mg and 1 mg respectively in phase 3a trials.

evidence_records.direct_quote: The use of semaglutide was associated with a higher relative risk and frequency of most adverse effects evaluated
evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide was associated with higher relative risk and frequency of most adverse effects evaluated.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Gastrointestinal tract disorders were reported most frequently and in greater proportions with continued semaglutide than placebo (41.9% vs 26.1%
evidence_records.statement: Gastrointestinal disorders occurred in 41.9% of semaglutide participants versus 26.1% on placebo during randomization.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Some of these errors have resulted in serious complications requiring hospitalization
evidence_records.statement: Some compounded semaglutide dosing errors led to serious complications requiring hospitalization.

evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist FDA-approved under three brand names: Ozempic, Rybelsus, and Wegovy.

index.title: Weight Loss Outcomes Associated With Semaglutide Treatment for Overweight or Obesity

evidence_records.direct_quote: patients receiving semaglutide, 2.4 mg, lost a mean of 6% of their weight by week 12 and 12% of their weight by week 28
evidence_records.statement: In STEP RCTs, patients on semaglutide 2.4 mg lost a mean of 6% of weight by week 12 and 12% by week 28.

evidence_records.direct_quote: In June 2021, the FDA approved subcutaneous semaglutide for long-term weight management,14 with higher doses of 1.7 and 2.4 mg once weekly.15
evidence_records.statement: FDA approved subcutaneous semaglutide (1.7 and 2.4 mg weekly) for long-term weight management in June 2021.

index.title: HIGHLIGHTS OF PRESCRIBING INFORMATION - WEGOVY (semaglutide)

evidence_records.direct_quote:
to reduce excess body weight and maintain weight reduction long term in: 

o Adults and pediatric patients aged 12 years and older with obesity.
evidence_records.statement: WEGOVY injection is indicated to reduce excess body weight and maintain weight reduction long term in adults with obesity.

evidence_records.direct_quote: to reduce the risk of major adverse cardiovascular (CV) events (CV death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, or non-fatal stroke) in adults with established CV disease and either obesity or overweight
evidence_records.statement: WEGOVY is indicated to reduce the risk of major adverse cardiovascular events in adults with established CV disease plus obesity or overweight.

index.title: WEGOVY (semaglutide) FDA Prescribing Information

evidence_records.direct_quote: adult patients with an initial body mass index (BMI) of • 30 kg/m 2 or greater (obesity) or • 27 kg/m 2 or greater (overweight)
evidence_records.statement: Wegovy is FDA-indicated for chronic weight management in adults with BMI >=30 or >=27 with a weight-related comorbidity.

evidence_records.direct_quote: In rodents, semaglutide causes dose-dependent and treatment-duration-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors at clinically relevant exposures
evidence_records.statement: The Wegovy boxed warning covers dose- and duration-dependent thyroid C-cell tumors observed in rodents.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Suicidal Behavior and Ideation: Monitor for depression or suicidal thoughts. Discontinue WEGOVY if symptoms develop
evidence_records.statement: Wegovy label warns of suicidal behavior and ideation, requiring monitoring for depression or suicidal thoughts.

index.title: WEGOVY (semaglutide) injection, for subcutaneous use - FDA Prescribing Information

evidence_records.direct_quote: to reduce the risk of major adverse cardiovascular (CV) events (CV death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, or non-fatal stroke)
evidence_records.statement: WEGOVY is indicated to reduce major adverse cardiovascular events in adults with established CV disease and either obesity or overweight.

evidence_records.direct_quote: reduce excess body weight and maintain weight reduction long term
evidence_records.statement: WEGOVY is indicated to reduce excess body weight and maintain weight reduction in adults and pediatric patients aged 12 and older with obesity.

index.title: WEGOVY (semaglutide) injection - FDA Prescribing Information

evidence_records.direct_quote: indicated as an adjunct to a reduced calorie diet and increased physical activity for chronic weight management in adult patients with an initial body mass index (BMI) of • 30 kg/m 2 or greater
evidence_records.statement: WEGOVY is indicated as an adjunct to a reduced calorie diet and increased physical activity for chronic weight management in adults with BMI of 30 kg/m2 or greater, or 27 kg/m2 with a weight-related comorbidity.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Acute Pancreatitis: Has occurred in clinical trials. Discontinue promptly if pancreatitis is suspected. Do not restart if pancreatitis is confirmed
evidence_records.statement: Acute pancreatitis has occurred in clinical trials of WEGOVY and treatment must be discontinued if pancreatitis is suspected.

index.title: Natural Products to Counteract the Epidemic of Cardiovascular and Metabolic Disorders

evidence_records.direct_quote: the prevalence of diabetes reported by the WHO was estimated to be 9% among adults aged 18+ years
evidence_records.statement: The WHO reported diabetes prevalence at 9% among adults aged 18+ in 2014, providing epidemiologic context for obesity-related metabolic disease.

index.title: Prescribing Information | Ozempic (semaglutide) injection

evidence_records.direct_quote: Do not share your Ozempic® pen with other people, even if the needle has been changed. You may give other people a serious infection or get a serious infection from them.
evidence_records.statement: Ozempic pens must not be shared between patients because of blood-borne pathogen transmission risk even with needle changes.

evidence_records.direct_quote: The most common side effects of Ozempic® may include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, stomach (abdominal) pain, and constipation.
evidence_records.statement: Common side effects of Ozempic include nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain, and constipation.

evidence_records.direct_quote: It is not known if Ozempic® is safe and effective for use in children.
evidence_records.statement: Ozempic safety and effectiveness in children has not been established by the manufacturer.

index.title: Glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonists and the potential risk of pancreatic carcinoma: a real-world pharmacovigilance study of post-marketing surveillance data

evidence_records.statement: Semaglutide showed a pancreatic carcinoma signal with ROR 7.43 (95% CI 5.22-10.57).

evidence_records.direct_quote: GLP-1RAs, except albiglutide, are associatedwith pancreatic carcinoma based on the pharmacovigilance study
evidence_records.statement: The authors conclude GLP-1RAs except albiglutide are associated with pancreatic carcinoma in pharmacovigilance data.

evidence_records.direct_quote: Liraglutide had the strongest signal detection (ROR 54.45, 95% CI 51.21-57.90; PRR 52.52, 95%CI 49.49-55.73; IC 5.59; EBGM 48.30)
evidence_records.statement: Liraglutide had the strongest pancreatic carcinoma signal (ROR 54.45, 95% CI 51.21-57.90).

index.title: RYBELSUS (semaglutide) tablets, for oral use - FDA Label

evidence_records.direct_quote: pancreatitis was reported as a serious adverse event in 6 RYBELSUS -treated patients (0.1 events per 100 patient years) versus 1 in comparator -treated patient
evidence_records.statement: In glycemic control trials, pancreatitis was reported as a serious adverse event in 6 RYBELSUS-treated patients (0.1 events per 100 patient years) versus 1 in comparator.

index.title: Impact of Bariatric Surgery on Left Ventricular Structure and Function

evidence_records.direct_quote: NT-proBNP significantly increased postoperatively from 200  pg/mL (61–636  pg/mL) to 392  pg/mL (162– 1720  pg/mL; P=0.005).
evidence_records.statement: NT-proBNP levels paradoxically increased postoperatively from 200 pg/mL to 392 pg/mL (P=0.005) in the 67 patients with available measurements.

index.title: Glucagon-like peptide 1 receptor agonists and cancer risk: the good, the bad and the unknown

evidence_records.direct_quote: the risk–benefit profile of GLP1RAs remains favourable in individuals with T2DM and obesity
evidence_records.statement: The overall risk-benefit profile of GLP-1 receptor agonists remains favourable in adults with T2DM and obesity.
